New post: Here's What to Know About Manhattan Tooth Extractions Before You Book
Nobody gets excited about needing a tooth extraction. Most people would prefer to keep every natural tooth they have for life, and dentists generally feel the same way. Still, there are situations where removing a tooth is the healthiest option for your smile and your long-term comfort.

If you've been researching tooth extraction in Manhattan, you've probably come across a lot of conflicting information. Some sources make the procedure sound intimidating, while others make it seem like no big deal. The reality falls somewhere in between. At Greene Street Dental, we believe patients feel more confident when they know exactly what to expect, so let's walk through some of the most common questions we hear before an extraction.
Not Every Problem Tooth Needs to Be Saved
Modern dentistry offers more ways than ever to preserve natural teeth. Treatments like crowns, root canals, and restorative procedures can often keep a damaged tooth functional for years.

Sometimes, though, saving a tooth isn't the best solution. Severe decay, advanced infection, significant fractures, or overcrowding may make extraction the healthier choice. In those cases, removing the tooth can help prevent additional complications and create a path toward a healthier smile.

The goal isn't simply to remove a problem, but to improve your overall oral health.
Common Reasons Dentists Recommend Extractions
There isn't one single

Your SoHo dentist has been right here for decades. From routine care to full smile makeovers, Greene Street Dental brings personalized, comfortable dentistry to the heart of Manhattan. New patients are welcome. Book your visit today and join our dental family.
Hot girl summer starts with a hot girl smile. Greene Street Dental offers flexible scheduling and modern care built for how SoHo actually lives. No waiting, no runaround. Book your visit today!

New post: Looking for a Trusted SoHo Dentist? Here’s What Sets Greene Street Dental Apart
Finding the right SoHo dentist can feel overwhelming. With so many dental practices to choose from in New York City, how do you know which one is the best fit for your needs? While credentials and services are certainly important, the overall patient experience can make all the difference when it comes to your long-term oral health.
At Greene Street Dental, we believe great dentistry goes beyond cleanings, exams, and treatments. It is about building relationships, earning trust, and creating a comfortable environment where patients feel genuinely cared for. Our team combines advanced dental technology with personalized attention to help every patient achieve a healthy, confident smile. Whether you're new to the area or simply looking for a dental home that feels different, here's what sets our practice apart.
Personalized Care Designed Around You
No two smiles are exactly alike, which is why we don't believe in one-size-fits-all dentistry. Every patient who walks through our doors has unique goals, concerns, and dental history. Our team takes the time to get to know you, understand your needs, and develop treatment recommendations that align with your lifestyle and priorities.
We know that some patients want to focus on preventive care, while others may be interested in cosmetic improvements or restorative treatments. Whatever brings you to our office, our goal is to provide clear

New post: Soho Dentists: Why the Same Treatment Doesn’t Always Lead to the Same Result
Two patients can come in for the exact same treatment with the same diagnosis, plan, and materials, yet walk away with slightly different experiences. It’s one of those things that can feel confusing at first, especially when everything looks identical on paper.

At Greene Street Dental, this is something we walk patients through often. Dentistry isn’t one-size-fits-all, even when the procedure is. Our dentists in Soho look beyond the treatment itself and focus on the factors that shape how your teeth respond, both during and after care.
Two Patients, One Plan: Where the Differences Begin
Imagine two patients receiving the same dental crown for a cracked tooth. Both had similar damage, both followed the same treatment steps, and both were good candidates.

One patient adjusts quickly. The crown feels natural, functions smoothly, and blends in right away. The other notices slight sensitivity at first or needs more time to feel fully comfortable.

The treatment didn’t change. The surrounding variables did. That’s where our dentists begin to look more closely.
Our Soho Dentists Explain What Actually Changes the Outcome
The difference in results rarely comes down to one big factor. More often, it’s a combination of small details that add up over time.
Biology Plays a Role
No two mouths behave exactly the same.

New post: Can Teeth Grinding Crack Teeth? When You Need Manhattan Dental Crowns
Waking up in the morning with a sore jaw and a headache? If this sounds familiar, chances are your teeth have been under a secret nightly attack: teeth grinding. While it might seem harmless, a subconscious habit you hardly notice, teeth grinding, or bruxism, can slowly turn your smile into a minefield of cracks and fractures. 

But fear not: with the right care, including dental crowns in Manhattan, even the most battle-worn teeth can be restored to strength and brilliance. At Greene Street Dental, we can tackle both the cracks and their source. 
When Your Smile Gets a Workout You Didn’t Sign Up for
Teeth grinding often happens unconsciously, either during the day or at night. While stress and anxiety are common triggers, it can also be linked to:

Sleep Disorders
Misaligned Teeth
Excessive Caffeine or Alcohol Intake
Certain Medications
Genetics
Temporomandibular Joint Disorder (TMJ)

Over time, this persistent grinding wears down enamel, weakens teeth, and can create tiny fractures that may eventually grow into noticeable cracks.

Even a small crack can escalate into a serious problem, causing pain and leaving teeth vulnerable to decay and infection. This is where our Manhattan dental crowns come into play, offering a durable, protective solution that restores both form and function.
Cracks in the Armor: How Bruxism Wears Down Teeth

New post: Lower Manhattan Cosmetic Dentistry: What Can Be Done for Valentine's Day
Valentine’s Day is just around the corner, and if your smile isn’t quite where you want it to be, now is the perfect time to act. Fortunately, Greene Street Dental offers a variety of quick, effective Lower Manhattan cosmetic dentistry treatments that can make a noticeable difference in just one or two visits. From brightening your teeth to freshening your breath, there’s still time to get your smile ready for the holiday.
Quick Treatments for a Valentine’s Day-Ready Smile
When time is short, it’s important to focus on general and aesthetic dentistry options that deliver fast results. At our office, you can access top-notch Lower Manhattan cosmetic dentistry services that can transform a smile in days or even hours. Let’s explore what can be done in time for Valentine’s Day.
1. Professional Teeth Whitening
A bright, sparkling smile is an instant confidence booster. In-office teeth whitening treatments can lighten your teeth several shades in just one appointment. 
When Should You Get Whitening?
Technically, you can get teeth whitening on the day of your big Valentine’s date. However, booking earlier can give your teeth more time to adjust. You might feel some sensitivity initially, and the color will also settle with time. 
2. Dental Bonding for Minor Imperfections
Chips, cracks, and small gaps don’t have to wait. Dental bonding uses a tooth-colored resin to repair and reshape teeth, often in a

New post: Dentist Soho: What Social Media Gets Wrong About Smiles
Social media has dramatically changed how people think about smiles. Ultra-white teeth, perfect symmetry, and dramatic before-and-after photos can make cosmetic dentistry look fast and universal. Over time, those images shape expectations about what a “good” smile should look like, even when the standards are unrealistic. What rarely shows up online is the planning and restraint required for results that look natural in real life.

At Greene Street Dental, patients often arrive with inspiration pulled straight from their feeds, along with understandable questions and mixed expectations. Working with our dentist in Soho helps translate visual inspiration into realistic options that fit real faces, daily life, and long-term oral health. Understanding where social media simplifies the story and where it leaves out important context makes those choices much clearer.
What Social Media Shows: One “Ideal” Smile Everyone Is Expected to Want
Scrolling through social media can make it feel like there is a single ideal smile everyone should aim for. Straight, bright, symmetrical teeth are often presented as the standard, regardless of age, facial structure, or natural tooth shape. Over time, that repetition can make natural variation feel like something that needs fixing.

This comparison can lead patients to focus more on trends than on what suits them personally. It can also create pressure to pursue changes that do not

New post: Dentist in SoHo on Modern Trends That Will Come Back to Bite You 
We all want that perfect, glittering smile that stops traffic on Broadway. However, in the rush to achieve instant aesthetic perfection, some trends are causing long-term damage that no filter can fix. At Greene Street Dental, we often see patients who have jumped on the latest viral bandwagon, only to face serious oral health consequences down the road.

Before you book that bargain flight for overseas veneers or pick up that "harmless" vape pen, you need the facts. Your smile is an investment, not a fast fashion accessory. Let's break down the modern habits that a top dentist in SoHo warns could cost you more than just money in the long run.
The "Veneer Tech" Trap: Crowns in Disguise
Social media is flooded with influencers showing off their "shark teeth"—shaved-down nubs ready for what they call veneers. Here is the scary truth: often, they aren't getting veneers at all. They are getting aggressive crowns that require removing a massive amount of healthy enamel.

True porcelain veneers are conservative. They require minimal preparation, preserving the structural integrity of your tooth. When you shave a tooth down to a peg, you are committing to a lifetime of expensive maintenance and increasing the risk of nerve damage or root canals.

Veneers: A thin shell covering the front surface, requiring minimal enamel removal.
Crowns: A cap covering the entire tooth, requiring significant reduction of natu

summary.snippet: "Wonderful, friendly staff and comfortable rooms with your choice of music."
"The service was excellent and the office is in a great location."
"Also was able to give me options moving forward with my treatment plan."

most_relevant.description: I got a very warm welcome into my first dental experience in the US. Olga, Steven, and Dr Janash were amazing in helping me feel safe in the cleaning of my plaques. They worked very efficiently throughout the painful process, and even took the time to educate me on the basics of teeth science. Would highly recommend a visit to anyone looking for a new dentist!

I thought the doctor & service was pretty good , but the exam room felt and looked pretty gross. Moldy ceiling tiles, it looked like the floor needed to be swept, it didn’t look fresh or updated like the waiting room did.  The sink looked like it had previous spit/listerine in it from someone before me, or at least was stained. Mind you they open at 9 and I had a 9am appointment.

The guy doing my X-rays was touching EVERYTHING including screens; drawers, doors, the remote, with gloves then putting those same  gloves in my mouth. I know they’ll say it’s hygienic but the xray  machine and everything else looked dirty and gross. I touched the remote myself which also felt gross. He could’ve changed his gloves. It just wasn’t up to my standards.

If they know everyone has to look up… why would you have dirty ceiling tiles ?

I visited Greene Street for the first time and had absolutely the best dental office experience. The hygienist, Olga was extremely professional, efficient and careful and I really appreciated Dr. Kim’s conservative approach. I felt like I was in great hands with their team!

First visit at Greene Street Dental and had the best visit! They were prompt, respectful, and communicative throughout the process. Olga was fantastic and made sure I was taken care of. Looking forward to my next visit!

This practice is no longer owned by the original Dentist, its now a private equity style office where they act like used car salesman selling you on services you don’t need. Avoid at all costs. They tried to tell my husband that he needed 3 root canals and when he went to another Dentist for a second opinion he found out he didn’t need the root canals. ( based on xray’s). When my son went to this office they tried to push several services on him including pushing him to have his wisdom teeth removed which is not necessary per his second opinion with 2 different dentists. Avoid this money grab!
I had a great experience at Greene St Dental and would recommend. Olga was thorough, kind and I will definitely be back.
Great experience getting my teeth cleaning by Olga she was very nice and professional. I would recommend to others to come to here.

Good , friendly and professional at administrative , c/service and surgeon levels .
Didn’t perform the procedure because didn’t feel ready and the price was a bit to high also not having insurance coverage
